stories.title: KPMG Women's PGA Championship Preview — Korda's Historic Grand Slam Bid at Hazeltine

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Nelly Korda arrives at Hazeltine this Thursday chasing history. She already won the Chevron and the US Women's Open in 2026. A KPMG win makes her only the 2nd woman to win 3 consecutive majors — last done by Inbee Park in 2013. #KPMG #LPGA

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Korda at 25/27 LPGA Hall of Fame points. A win at the KPMG puts her at 26. One more after that — she's in. The biggest week of her career starts Thursday at Hazeltine. $13M purse. #LPGA #NellyKorda

research.facts:
KPMG Women's PGA Preview — Korda Grand Slam
**Source:** LPGA.com, GolfDigest, GolfChannel, StartTribune, JustWomensSports (June 2026)
- Tournament: KPMG Women's PGA Championship, June 25–28, Hazeltine National Golf Club, Chaska, Minnesota
- Purse: $13 million (record for women's golf, reclaims richest event title)
- Korda's 2026 season: 9 starts, 4 wins, worst finish T-8 in individual event
- Korda won Chevron Championship in April (5-shot win) + US Women's Open (1-shot win)
- Grand Slam context: Only Inbee Park in 2013 (ANA Inspiration + KPMG Women's PGA) won consecutive majors in women's golf modern era
- Korda at Hazeltine 2019: finished T-3 (last time tournament was held here)
- Key competitors: Atthaya "Jeeno" Thitikul (No. 2 world ranking), Minjee Lee (defending champion), Hannah Green (3 career majors, 11 LPGA titles), Inbee Park
- Hannah Green: won KPMG 2019 at Hazeltine for first LPGA title; 2 top-5s before March 2026, no 2026 wins yet
- Thitikul: 5 LPGA wins, no major; overdue for breakthrough
- Format: 72-hole stroke play, no cut
- Confidence: HIGH

stories.title: Travelers Championship Preview — Scheffler Favored as PGA Tour's Final Signature Event Tees Up

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: The PGA Tour's final signature event of 2026 starts Thursday. $20M purse. $3.6M to the winner. Scheffler is +480. Jordan Spieth (2017 champ) is in. Wyndham Clark comes off winning the US Open. No McIlroy. Let's go. ⛳ #TravelersChampionship

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Scottie Scheffler: No. 1 in the world. 8 top-5 finishes in 13 starts this season. Still looking for his 2nd win of 2026. The Travelers Championship at TPC River Highlands starts Thursday. Scheffler's on the clock. ⛳ #TravelersChampionship #PGATour

research.facts:
Travelers Championship Preview
**Source:** GolfChannel, PGATour.com, Yahoo Sports, CBS Sports, DraftKings Network (June 2026)
- Tournament: Travelers Championship (final PGA Tour signature event of 2026)
- Dates: June 25–28, TPC River Highlands, Cromwell, Connecticut
- Purse: $20 million; winner's share: $3.6 million
- Field: 72 players, no cut; 22 of top 25 in world
- McIlroy skipping to prepare for links run (Open Championship prep)
- Scheffler: +480 favorite (FanDuel); world No. 1; 2024 Travelers champion; 8 top-5s in 13 starts 2026; seeking 2nd win of season
- Fleetwood: +1600; Schauffele: +1800; Ludvig Aberg: +2000
- Jordan Spieth in field (2017 Travelers champ, course history)
- US Open champion Wyndham Clark enters; Sam Burns (runner-up US Open) also in field
- Past champions: Scheffler (2024), Keegan Bradley (defending 2025 champion looking to repeat)
- TPC River Highlands: relatively low course rating, scoring-friendly, birdies-galore format
- Confidence: HIGH (multiple sources)

stories.title: LIV Golf $400M Crisis — PIF Loans Fund Operations While Future Hangs in Balance

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: LIV Golf has spent over $6B since 2022. Now they're running on Saudi PIF loans just to finish the 2026 season. They need $400M in outside investment — and can't find it. Four events left. Two may be cancelled. #LIVGolf #Golf

research.facts:
LIV Golf Financial Crisis
**Source:** Field Level Media, Golf Monthly, Fox News/OutKick, DailyClubGolf (June 2026)
- LIV Golf running on Saudi PIF loans (debenture agreement) — not capital
- PIF announced in April: would bankroll LIV only through end of 2026 season
- Total PIF spend on LIV since 2022: more than $6 billion
- LIV monthly spend: ~$100 million
- 2026 funding received: $333 million (only enough for 3-4 months)
- Seeking $400 million+ in outside investment; has not secured it
- 4 events remain in 2026: UK (July 23-26), New York (Aug 6-9), Indianapolis (Aug 20-23), $40M Team Championship Michigan (Aug 27-30)
- Louisiana event cancelled (cited summer heat)
- 2027 proposed: 10 events (5 "team majors" in established markets + 5 US-based "team signature events")
- Prize money likely dropping from $32.3M per event
- Confidence: HIGH (multiple financial reports, company filings)

stories.title: Korda's Hall of Fame Clock — KPMG Win Would Put Her One Away from Enshrinement

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: The math on Nelly Korda's LPGA Hall of Fame journey: she needs 27 pts. She has 25. A KPMG win = 26. One more after that — she's enshrined. She turns 28 this year. This might be the fastest path to the HoF in LPGA history. #LPGA #NellyKorda

research.facts:
Korda Hall of Fame Quest
**Source:** LPGA.com, GolfChannel, JustWomensSports, Yahoo Sports (June 2026)
- Korda LPGA Hall of Fame points: currently 25 of 27 required points
- After Chevron win: moved to 22/27 (per JustWomensSports); after US Women's Open: now 25/27 (per LPGA.com)
- Note: LPGA HoF = 27 points required + 10-year LPGA membership
- KPMG win = additional point → 26/27
- Then needs one more point to reach 27 and achieve eligibility
- Points breakdown (approx.): LPGA major wins = 2 pts each; Solheim Cup wins = 1 pt each; other LPGA victories = 1 pt each
- Korda turned pro 2016; eligible for 10-year membership 2026
- She's age 27 with a realistic path to enshrinement this season
- Grand Slam: winning Chevron + US Women's Open + KPMG + (Evian OR AIG Women's Open) = Women's Grand Slam for 2026
- Confidence: HIGH (LPGA official source)

stories.title: Wyndham Clark — US Open Champion Two Years Apart, Now Enters Travelers at TPC River Highlands

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Wyndham Clark just won the 2026 US Open wire-to-wire at Shinnecock Hills. He also won the 2023 US Open. That's two US Open titles — and now he heads straight to the Travelers Championship starting Thursday. What a stretch. ⛳🏆 #USOpen #TravelersChampionship

research.facts:
Wyndham Clark US Open 2026 Recap
**Source:** Golf Channel, Golf Digest, pipeline story history (June 22, 2026)
- Clark won 2026 US Open at Shinnecock Hills, NY
- Score: -4 for tournament; won by 1 shot over Sam Burns
- Burns shot 73 on Sunday (-1 Sunday finish); Clark held the lead
- Round 1: Clark shot 64 — second-lowest round in Shinnecock Hills history (36-hole tournament record)
- Wire-to-wire win — first US Open wire-to-wire since Martin Kaymer 2014
- 9th wire-to-wire US Open in history
- Gallery described as hostile at Shinnecock — rowdy crowd, Clark held composure
- Clark also won 2023 US Open (his first major)
- Now 2-time US Open champion
- Scheffler Grand Slam bid ended (was 6 back entering Sunday)
- Clark entering Travelers Championship (June 25-28)
- Confidence: HIGH (pipeline history + public reporting)
